content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach makes one explicit, useful read at turn 6 ('I notice you've mentioned how good things are at home a few times now') — this is observation language that names a linguistic pattern (the repeated reassurance / happiness filter) and it shifts the client into a small admission via laugh. That's a real read, named in the moment. However, the coach misses multiple subsequent somatic/energetic cues that are visibly available in the transcript: the head tilt at turn 5, the flicker behind the smile at turn 11, the self-confirming nod at turn 13, the settling-back gesture. None of these are named. The coach's later questions ('what's your thing?', 'what do you work around?', 'what was that like?') are good content-level probes but they are blind — not grounded in a named read. The defendedness-as-identity-signal ('I'd rather have what we have...', 'that's what a good relationship looks like') is not surfaced. One specific read plus several missed visible cues puts this squarely in the 2 band — better than content-only, short of consistent timely reads.",
  "evidence_turns": [6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Topic-level redirect; no read named."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening question, not grounded in an observation."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Explicit linguistic read using 'I notice' — names the repetition of 'how good things are.' Lands; client laughs and partially concedes."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Misses the laugh and the deflection-onto-others; asks a content question instead of naming what just shifted."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Good probe but doesn't name the defensive framing ('every relationship has its thing') that prompted it."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Misses the flicker behind the smile and the settling-back at turn 11 — visible somatic cues left unnamed."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Misses the self-confirming nod and the doctrinal phrasing ('that's what a good relationship looks like'); question proceeds without naming the bracing."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-happiness-filter"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["walking-on-eggshells", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "One clear, well-timed read (turn 6) prevents a 1; absence of somatic/defensive reads at turns 11 and 13 prevents a 3."
}
